sections.paragraph:
f. wheat, in sing. especially as growing in the fields. Exo 9:32 Deu 8:8 Job 31:40 Isa 28:25 Joe 1:11. The expression חֵלֶב חִטָּה Psal. 81:17 , fat of wheat, is, however, to be explained of grains of wheat, and so חֵלֶכ בִּלְיֹות חִטָּה fat of kidneys of wheat, Deu 32:14 fat thus used denoting the medulla or flour of the wheat, μυελὸν ἀνδρῶν; it is also called חֵלֶב חִטִּים Psa 147:14.

Plural חִטִּים grains of wheat (the sing. is found applied to one grain, חִטָּה, Mishn. Chelaim i. § 9), זָרַע חִטִּים Jer 12:13 קְצִיר חִטִּים Gen 30:14 דּוּשׁ חִמִּים 1Ch 21:20 כֹּר חִטִּים 2Ch 27:5. By a Chaldaism חִמִּין Eze 4:9.

In the cognate languages it is خِزْطَةُ, خنْطُ Chald. חִנְטִין, and some on this account regard חָנַט to season, as its root. But, however, the letter Nun may be inserted as originating in Teth doubled, so that the root may be חט. [In Thes. it is put under חָנַט, where it seems to belong.] The Gr. σῖτος, wheat, appears to answer to this word, the aspirate being changed into a sibilant. [“Bohlen compares Sanscr. godhuma, wheat, so called from its yellow colour. Pers. كتندم.”]
